content.full_text:
State of California

GOVERNOR'S OFFICE OF PLANNING AND RESEARCH
1400 TENTH STREET
SACRAMENTO 95814

PETE WILSON
GOVERNOR

(916) 322-3170

RICHARD SYBERT
DIRECTOR

February 25, 1992


Mr. Ben Davidian, Chair
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Davidian:

RE: Applicability of FPPC Filing Requirements to Members of the
Geographic Information Task Force

I respectfully request an opinion on the necessity for members
of the recently constituted Geographic Information Task Force
(created by Chapter 782, Statutes of 1990 and constituted by
Executive Order W-22-92) to file economic disclosure statements.
This opinion is requested prior to the first meeting of the Task
Force, which will be scheduled for mid-March 1992.

The Task Force consists of thirteen (13) voting and six (6) non-
voting members. Its mandate is to review current geographic
information resources and make recommendations to improve data
standards, accuracy, compatibility, and exchange. Its recommenda-
tions are to be submitted to the Governor and legislature by October
1, 1992. The Task Force is strictly advisory and has no regulatory
or program implementation responsibilities. A copy of the statute
and executive order that created and constituted the Task Force are
attached.

Because the first meeting will be held very shortly, I will
appreciate your giving this request you immediate attention. If you
need any additional information, please call me at (916) 322-3170.

Sincerely,

Ben Williams
Deputy Director
Administrative Services

PAGE TWO

- the Director of the Office of Information Technology within the
Department of Finance.

3. The Chairman of the Task Force shall request participation from the
following representatives:

- the California Land Surveyors' Association, which shall submit the
names of five nominees, one of whom shall be selected by the
Chairman; and

- the California Council of Civil Engineers and Land Surveyors, which
shall submit the names of five nominees, one of whom shall be
selected by the Chairman; and

- the Chief of the Mapping Division of the United States Geological
Survey; and

- the State Director of the Bureau of Land Management; and

- the Regional Forester of the United States Forest Service; and

- the Director of the Center for Oceanic Analysis and Prediction of
the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration; and

- a Member of the State Board of Equalization; and

- representatives of the University of California and California State
University.

4. Staff for the task force shall be provided by the Stephen F. Teale Data
Center and other personnel, at the request of the Chairman or other
members of the task force.

5. The task force shall consider any standards currently under development
by professional associations and the federal government in its
recommendations.

6. The task force shall perform its work and submit its report to the
Governor and the Legislature by October 1, 1992. The work and the report
shall fulfill the requirements enumerated in Chapter 782, Statutes of
1991, and shall emphasize recommendations to improve utilization,
accuracy, cost-effectiveness, and exchange of geographic information.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF I have hereunto set my hand
and caused the Great Seal of the State of
California to be affixed this 7th day of
February 1992.

[signature]
Governor of California

ATTEST:

[signature]
Secretary of State

EXECUTIVE DEPARTMENT
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

EXECUTIVE ORDER W-22-92

WHEREAS, the State of California is blessed with extraordinary and diverse
human and natural resources, geography and climate; and

WHEREAS, the wise management of the land, waters and other resources, risk
assessment, property management, and decision-making at all levels of government
and in the private sector rely heavily upon geographic information and various
mapping and surveying techniques; and

WHEREAS, the State of California has the unique opportunity to enhance the
protection of California's resources in a more cost-effective manner and can make
the process of environmental regulation more efficient and predictable through the
use of computer technology; and

WHEREAS, the development of statewide, compatible, multipurpose geographic
information systems can be a key tool in such functions; and

WHEREAS, many public and private entities in California have similar needs and
overlapping requirements for geographic information and are now using computers to
manage geographic information; and

WHEREAS, the development, use, maintenance, and exchange of computer-based
geographic information inevitably gives rise to different issues, opportunities,
and problems; and

WHEREAS, legislation (Chapter 782, Statutes of 1991) was recently enacted
establishing a Geographic Information Task Force to develop recommendations with
regard to the above issues;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, PETE WILSON, Governor of the State of California, by virtue
of the power and authority vested in me by the Constitution and statutes of the
State of California, do hereby issue this order to become effective immediately:

1.    The Director of the Office of Planning and Research is hereby
     designated Chairman of the Geographic Information Task Force and shall
     convene the initial meeting of the Task Force at the earliest practical
     date.

2.    The following State representatives shall also serve on the Task Force:

     -    the Director of the Stephen P. Tesla Data Center; and

     -    the Secretary of the Business, Transportation and Housing Agency;
          and

     -    the Secretary of the Resource Agency; and

     -    the Secretary of the California Environmental Protection Agency; and
